Same outward behavior as s05 from the user's perspective. The diff is on
disk: there's now a `<workspace>/` directory (default
`~/.learn-nanobot/agents/default/`) with two files after one turn, and
optional Markdown files for curated memory.

$ go run . -workspace /tmp/agent-demo -session demo "list .go files in this directory"
bash_tool.go bus.go main.go memory.go provider.go provider_anthropic.go provider_fake.go registry.go runner.go session.go tools.go

$ ls /tmp/agent-demo
.cursor
history.jsonl

$ cat /tmp/agent-demo/.cursor
2

$ wc -l /tmp/agent-demo/history.jsonl
2 history.jsonl

$ head -1 /tmp/agent-demo/history.jsonl | python3 -m json.tool
{
    "ts": "2026-05-09T...",
    "role": "user",
    "content": "list .go files in this directory"
}

The cursor is 2 because main appends two HistoryEntry rows per turn:
the user prompt and the assistant's final reply. The assistant row also
carries `tools_used` and `usage` if the LLM called tools.

If you re-run with the same -workspace, history.jsonl grows by 2 lines
each turn and .cursor advances accordingly. MEMORY.md / SOUL.md / USER.md
remain absent until s10's Consolidator (or App. A's Dream) writes them.

If your run errors with "iteration 0: provider error http_401" — your
ANTHROPIC_API_KEY is wrong. Same Runner-level error message as s05.

If "workspace: …": -workspace is invalid. Default is
~/.learn-nanobot/agents/default; pass an absolute path to override.

If history.jsonl already exists from a previous run, AppendHistory just
adds to the bottom and writes the new line count to .cursor. There is no
"erase" path in MemoryStore — the only mutation surface is AppendHistory
plus the three atomic-replace WriteMemory variants.

Concurrent behavior:

$ go test -count=1 -race -run TestMemoryStore_AppendHistory_Concurrent_ConsistentLines ./...
PASS — 10 goroutines append concurrently; final history.jsonl has exactly
10 lines and every line parses as JSON.
